name: Ginger Bread (Medieval/elizabethan)
recipeInstructions:
Course Ginger Bread—Take a quart of honey clarified, and seeth it till it be brown, and if it be thick, put to it a dash of water. Then take fine crumbs of white bread grated, and put to it, and stir it well. When it is almost cold, put to it the powder of Ginger, Cloves, Cinnamon, and a little Licorice and Anise seeds. Knead it, and put it into a mold and press it. Some use to put to it also a little Pepper, but that is according unto taste and pleasure.
1. In the top of a double boiler, heat honey. Add spices except anise seeds, and stir to blend.
2. Add bread crumbs and mix thoroughly. Cover and cook over medium heat for 15 minutes. The mixture should be thick and moist.
3. Place gingerbread on a large sheet of waxed paper. Fold up the sides of the paper and mold dough into a small rectangular shape.
4. Sprinkle anise seeds on top and press them gently into dough with the side of a knife.
5. Cover and refrigerate for 2 hours.
6. Serve gingerbread at room temperature in thin slices.
